name: Obscene acts and songs
penalty: imprisonment up to 3 months, fine up to ₹200
statute_text: Whoever to the annoyance of others—(a) does any obscene act in any public place, or (b) sings, recites or utters any obscene song, ballad or words, in or near any public place, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to two hundred rupees, or with both.
summary: Prohibits obscene acts or songs in public places

name: Voluntarily causing hurt
penalty: imprisonment up to 1 month, fine up to ₹250
statute_text: Whoever, except in the case provided by law, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to two hundred and fifty rupees, or with both.
summary: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt to others

name: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
penalty: imprisonment up to 7 years, fine, or both
statute_text: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed and is capable of being converted into a valuable security, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
summary: Addresses fraud, cheating, and deceptive practices

name: Cruelty by husband or his relatives
penalty: imprisonment up to 3 years, fine up to ₹100,000, or both
statute_text: Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years and shall also be liable to fine which may extend to one hundred thousand rupees.
summary: Addresses domestic violence and cruelty against wives

name: Criminal intimidation
penalty: imprisonment up to 2 years, fine up to ₹250, or both
statute_text: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property, or to the person or reputation of anyone in whom that person is interested, with intent to cause alarm to that person, or to cause that person to do any act which he is not legally bound to do, or to omit to do any act which that person is legally entitled to do, as the means of avoiding the execution of such threat, commits criminal intimidation.

name: Intentional insult with knowledge that it will provoke breach of peace
penalty: imprisonment up to 2 years, fine up to ₹250, or both
statute_text: Whoever intentionally insults, and thereby causes or knows that he is likely thereby to cause, any person to break the public peace or to commit any other offence, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine which may extend to two hundred and fifty rupees, or with both.
summary: Addresses insults causing breach of peace

name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
penalty: imprisonment up to 3 years, fine up to ₹20,000, or both
statute_text: The Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005 is an act of the Indian Parliament enacted to protect women from abuse in relationships. Any male adult who has or has had an intimate relationship with the aggrieved person and resides in the same household is liable.
summary: Comprehensive law protecting women from domestic violence

name: Dowry Prohibition Act, 1961
penalty: imprisonment up to 5 years, fine up to ₹15,000, or both
statute_text: Any person who gives or takes or abets the giving or taking of dowry shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than five years, and with fine which shall not be less than fifteen thousand rupees or fifteen times the amount of dowry, whichever is more.
summary: Prohibits giving or taking dowry in marriage

name: Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016
statute_text: The Aadhaar Act provides for targeted delivery of financial and other subsidies, benefits and services using Aadhaar number. Unauthorized disclosure of information is a criminal offense.
summary: Protects against misuse of personal identity information

name: Hindu Succession Act, 1956
statute_text: This act regulates succession and inheritance of property among Hindus, Buddhists, Sikhs, and Jains. It prescribes the order of succession and the rights of various heirs to inherited property.
summary: Governs inheritance and succession of property

name: Hindu Marriage Act, 1955
statute_text: This act regulates the solemnization, validity, grounds for divorce, and other matters relating to Hindu marriages..
summary: Governs marriage, divorce and matrimonial relations
